source: Caramuel, Primus Calamus (1663), p.452 (scan idx 451), TABVLA XVI
family: labyrinthus-radialis (polar wheel: concentric rings x radial spokes; cartouche COELVM LILIVET DENSE; centre hub 'Lilia PHOEB. amat')
title_diplomatic: COELVM LILIVET DENSE — Lilia PHOEBVS amat
topology_note: First POLAR plate. Readings run two ways: SPOKE (hub -> rim along one radius) and RING (circumferentially within one ring). Verse forms as the reader spirals/rotates. ~36 spokes x ~6 rings; only inner-ring anchors transcribed with confidence, outer rings flagged.

author_combinatorial_claim.diplomatic: (no explicit numeral legible on this plate)
author_combinatorial_claim.note: radial labyrinth; multiplicity arises from spoke x ring permutation

figure.emblem_layer_note: Surrounded by a star-field and four planetary-orbit roundels (Copernican/Ptolemaic diagrams) in the corners; mitres and arms in the cartouche header.

calibration_role: First polar-topology plate — exercises the FigureGraph on non-rectangular, non-triangular geometry (rings+spokes). Tests that DETECT/ductus handle a substrate whose 'rows' are curved.
